nodes.title: ATProto meetup in Brooklyn - met Henry Zoo, saw his receipt printer printing Bluesky mentions
Bought Epson TM-M50 (same model as Henry Zoo) - bluetooth/wifi model
Bluetooth and WiFi wouldn't work - had to get USB B cable
Where to run the printer: work laptop vs dedicated hardware
First printing: Python script piping to lp command via CUPS - echo hello | lp -d EPSON_TM_m50
Blog already consumes entire Bluesky firehose live - can filter for @bobbby.online and #bobby mentions
Friday night party - printed tons of Bluesky mentions, addicting, messages kept coming
Print images on thermal printer - started with a wedding photo
Thermal printers speak ESC/POS (Epson 1980s protocol) - can only render black or white dots, no gray
Implemented Floyd-Steinberg dithering - error diffusion: 7/16 right, 3/16 lower-left, 5/16 below, 1/16 lower-right
Built image pipeline: PIL load -> resize to 576/640px -> grayscale -> dither -> pack 8px/byte MSB -> GS v 0 raster command
Wedding photo splitter scripts: split_wedding.py, split_wedding_complete.py, wedding_poster.py - splits large image into vertical strips
ReceiptPrinter class emerged, then FullWidthPrinter subclass - wedding scripts deleted but printer code stayed and grew
Extract printer code to dedicated repo: Hermes (messenger of the gods)
Added pyusb direct USB communication - vendor 0x04b8, product 0x0202, kernel driver detachment, 4096-byte chunking
Two connection strategies in same codebase: CUPS (lp command) and pyusb direct USB - first worked, second was better, never removed first
Built polling pipeline: Flask -> Job Queue (persistent JSON) -> PrintWorker (threaded) -> Printer Interface -> CUPS
Job queue is genuinely good: UUIDs, status tracking, 3 retries, persistent JSON survives crashes, stale job recovery, keeps last 100 jobs
Give the receipt printer a phone number - Twilio SMS/MMS
Twilio integration: +1(855)578-6365 - text or MMS prints on thermal printer, ngrok tunnels webhooks to local Flask API
Wrote _init_twilio() and _send_confirmation() - would text back confirming message was printed
Tested with test_mms.py and test_api.py - it worked. Then deleted test files in commit 'you never shoulda been here' and never wired _init_twilio into worker loop
Twilio SID and Auth Token hardcoded in 8 files. .env.example has real credentials. Personal phone in 4 places. Flask API token committed to git
image_width contradiction: receipt_printer.py sets 640, custom_printer.py overrides to 576, printer_interface.py overrides to 576 again. Value 576 hardcoded 25+ places. No single source of truth
Build receipt printing into the blog's Elixir/Phoenix codebase
Created receipt_messages table, Ecto schema, LiveView form, legacy API controller, thermal printer module with PNG decoder and Floyd-Steinberg dithering - 1110 lines in one commit
SimplePNG: minimal PNG decoder written from scratch - no external deps. Validates PNG signature, parses IHDR/IDAT/IEND, zlib decompression, scanline filter processing, supports grayscale/RGB/RGBA
ImageProcessor: Floyd-Steinberg dithering reimplemented in Elixir using Erlang :array. Error distribution to 4 neighbors, pixel-to-byte packing, ESC/POS format output
WIP on LiveView form, then 'oops' fix - iterating on the web form rapidly
Added image_data binary column and image_content_type to receipt_messages table
Built Rust ESC/POS printer emulator: TCP server on port 9100 that parses and renders ESC/POS commands without real hardware
Rust emulator: full ESC/POS parser including text formatting, alignment, barcode commands, image raster commands. Outputs to files in printed_receipts/ directory
Added authenticated API endpoints, Python polling service, Elixir ReceiptBuilder/NetworkClient/EscPos/VirtualPrinter/Demo/Examples modules, ESC/POS emulator, shell scripts - 2681 lines
Configured API token, .env.receipt_printer, LaunchAgent plist, receipt_poller.sh - production deployment setup
Messages flowing: 'get the messages going right' - receipt_messages context module working with pending/printed/failed status machine

Massive restructure: removed Rust emulator, removed Elixir receipt_printer lib modules (demo, esc_pos, examples, network_client, receipt_builder, virtual_printer), added Python receipt_printer.py (423 lines), added Elixir GenServer printer service, added Jetstream code - net -1266 lines

The a90a468 commit is revealing: deleted the Rust emulator AND the Elixir receipt_printer library modules, replaced with a Python receipt_printer.py (full ESC/POS class with 3 dithering algorithms) and Elixir GenServer that polls pending messages

receipt_printer.py at this point: 423 lines, ReceiptPrinter class with CUPS integration, Floyd-Steinberg + ordered + threshold dithering, image width 640px, 42-char line wrap. The Python version becomes the reference implementation
Photo booth: Pi + camera + keyboard in shoebox, press ENTER to take photo and print dithered receipt
Started photo_booth/ repo - copy-pasted printer files from Hermes repo. Receipt with 3 shots and SHA-256 hash of each image
Paths broke immediately on Pi (5:33 PM). Script tripled to +329 lines with debugging (6:48 PM). Debugging the debugging (7:38 PM)
10:44 PM commit 'well': discovered Pi can't have camera AND printer USB at same time - one USB port. Solution: SWAP TO PRINTER NOW! prompt

Now TWO repos with receipt_printer.py, custom_printer.py, enhance_receipt_image.py - 95% identical. Photo booth has more aggressive enhancement: brightness 1.3 vs 1.1, contrast 1.5 vs 1.2, plus sharpening
Added adaptive_dither.py: 3 copy-pasted Floyd-Steinberg loops (lowlight/auto/bright) with different gamma/threshold. enhance_receipt_image.py grew to 475 lines with lowlight_enhance/daylight_enhance

Script peaked at 885 lines in 22 hours (25 commits). 873-line bash script that generates Python inline, manages kernel modules, handles camera, drives printer, begs you to swap USB cables. It WORKED at an event.
print_session_debug(): when something goes wrong, renders debug log as IMAGE and prints it on receipt printer. Because there's no screen. The printer IS the debugger.
The cats broke the printer. Months pass. Two repos sit untouched. Code rots.
Wife bought new printer: EPSON TM-T88VI replacing original TM-M50. Text printing came back immediately.
Website form works again. Messages printing: 'here comes the sun', 'Pssssst Cmere (Fart noise)', 'the angels have the phone box'
Zalgo text crashed the printer. 3 retries, all failed. Sender apologized: 'Sent a message with Zalgo text only to panic after the fact'
96 jobs in queue. 71 completed. 25 failed. 26% failure rate. Images won't print on new hardware. Twilio phone number is dead.
Fix compiler warnings: deprecations, unused vars, imports across all receipt/thermal printer Elixir modules
Clean up printer shell scripts: receipt_poller.sh, setup_receipt_service.sh, start_printer.sh, start_receipt_printer.sh
Update Python receipt_printer.py and receipt_printer_service.py
Add OS window chrome styling to all pages including receipt_message_live
Build The Rooted in Good Method Platform
nodes.metadata_json: {"confidence":95,"prompt":"I have a parenting book coming out called The Rooted in Good Method. It's about the importance of having touchpoints with your children. The 3 touchpoints are: 1) Touch (20 seconds of physical connection - hugs, hand holding) 2) Listen (10 minutes of active listening) 3) Quality Time (20 minutes of undivided attention). We need a full website platform with: e-commerce for the book and supplementary materials, an 8-week guided journey for parents who bought the book, a community hub around a Facebook group with AA-style weekly support meetings, and an admin marketing dashboard for managing social media content, shareable branded images, and campaign planning. We also want it to have a light simple theme - light greens, baby blues, blush pink, light yellows. Design principles: functional core, imperative shell, push complexity to the edges. TEST EVERYTHING, use vitest."}

Phase 8: Polish - animations, mobile responsiveness, SEO
Audited all layouts and pages for mobile responsiveness
Admin layout had CRITICAL mobile issue: fixed w-64 sidebar with no toggle, ml-64 margin with no responsive breakpoint - completely broken on phones
Dashboard layout, Navigation, Footer, all other pages already had proper responsive handling (md: breakpoints, hamburger menus, flex-wrap)

Added robots.txt
Added CSS animations: fade-in, fade-in-up, stagger children, hover-lift, focus-visible ring

Polish complete: mobile-responsive admin sidebar, loading spinners for all route groups, full SEO metadata with OG/Twitter cards, subtle animations, keyboard accessibility focus rings. Build: 21 routes clean. Tests: 169/169 green.

Architecture: 4-layer system - UI Components (thin display, props in/events out) → Server Actions (thin wrappers calling core + DB) → src/core/ (ALL business logic, pure functions, zero deps) → src/types/ (shared types, no runtime). This means 100% of business logic is testable without mocks.

Route groups organize the app: (marketing) for public pages with Nav+Footer, (shop) for e-commerce with Nav+Footer, (dashboard) for authenticated journey with sidebar, (admin) for admin-only with dark sidebar. Each group has its own layout.tsx and loading.tsx.

Server vs Client component boundary: Server components (pages) handle data fetching via Prisma and pass serialized plain objects to client components. Client components handle interactivity (useState, onClick, timers). Key lesson: functions (like Lucide icons) cannot cross this boundary.

Platform Status: 21 routes, 169 tests, clean build. Full stack: landing page, about, community (FB group + meetings + shareable gallery), shop (4 products, cart, mock checkout), 8-week guided journey (timers, streaks, reflections), admin dashboard (calendar, posts, shareable creator, campaigns, users). Auth with role-based access. Mobile responsive. SEO optimized.

Feb 12 was 10 commits in one day on receipt_message_live.ex alone - classic 'launching to the public' energy. 7 of 10 commits are LiveView form fixes/polish

LANGUAGE SPLIT: Python (receipt_printer.py, receipt_printer_service.py) does actual ESC/POS + image processing. Elixir (Phoenix) handles web form, API, database, GenServer polling. Rust emulator was built and deleted in 2 days.

DITHERING COUNT: 6+ implementations total. Python: Floyd-Steinberg + ordered + threshold in receipt_printer.py. Elixir: Floyd-Steinberg in image_processor.ex. Photo booth: 3 variants (lowlight/auto/bright) in adaptive_dither.py. All slightly different constants.

CONNECTION STRATEGIES: 3 incompatible methods. 1) CUPS via lp command (original + photo booth). 2) pyusb direct USB with vendor/product ID (Hermes). 3) TCP socket to 127.0.0.1:9100 (blog's Python service + Elixir GenServer)

THE ELIXIR GRAVEYARD: receipt_printer/ modules (demo.ex, esc_pos.ex, examples.ex, network_client.ex, receipt_builder.ex, virtual_printer.ex) were created in c8920d0 on Aug 27 and DELETED in a90a468 on Aug 29 - lived 2 days. But receipt_printer_emulator.ex survived.

DATABASE SCHEMA: receipt_messages table with status machine (pending->printed/failed). Fields: content, sender_name, sender_ip, image_url, image_data (binary), image_content_type, printed_at. Two migrations, one creating table, one adding image binary column.

DEPLOYMENT: 3 overlapping service configs. 1) receipt-printer.service (systemd, runs Rust emulator binary). 2) com.bobbby.receipt-printer.plist (macOS LaunchAgent, runs receipt_poller.sh). 3) .env.receipt_printer (API_URL=https://www.bobbby.online, PRINTER_HOST=127.0.0.1:9100)

THE BLOG POST: 'Vibe Coding Rescue Missions' (latest_post.md, 300 lines) tells the full story as a technical narrative. Planned rescue: 1) Extract one printer library 2) Unify image pipeline 3) Kill hardcoded paths/creds 4) Fix dead Twilio 5) Get images printing on new printer 6) Replace 873-line bash script

THE MESS ISN'T INCOMPETENCE, IT'S VELOCITY: Good decisions made in isolation under time pressure, never reconciled. Wedding project, event photo booth, Bluesky party - each needed to work RIGHT NOW. Debt accumulated from good decisions in different repos

CURRENT STATE (Feb 2026): Text printing works. Web form works. Images DON'T print on new TM-T88VI. Twilio phone number is dead. receipt_printer_emulator.ex (Elixir ESC/POS emulator with HTML output) exists but is disconnected from everything. SimplePNG decoder exists but was never really used in production pipeline.

FILE INVENTORY in blog repo today: receipt_printer.py (Python ESC/POS class, 640px, CUPS), receipt_printer_service.py (Python API poller, TCP socket, 576px), thermal_printer.ex (Elixir image handler), image_processor.ex (Elixir Floyd-Steinberg), simple_png.ex (Elixir PNG decoder), receipt_printer_service.ex (Elixir GenServer poller), receipt_printer_emulator.ex (Elixir ESC/POS emulator), message_handler.ex (Elixir receipt formatter)

TWO PRINT WIDTHS in the SAME repo: receipt_printer.py uses 640px (80mm at 203dpi), receipt_printer_service.py uses 576px. message_handler.ex wraps at 44 chars (2-space indent on 48-char line). receipt_printer_service.py wraps at 44 chars. receipt_printer.py wraps at 42 chars. Three different wrap widths.

nodes.title: Dual supervision tree with rescue branch is a code smell
nodes.description: Blog.Application.start/2 has the children list duplicated in both the normal path and the rescue ArgumentError path. When adding new children to the supervision tree, they must be added to BOTH branches or they silently fail to start on hot reload. This pattern exists because ETS table creation can raise ArgumentError if tables already exist, and the entire supervisor startup wraps in try/rescue.

nodes.title: PubSub is the nervous system — 10+ topics routing data between features
nodes.description: Phoenix PubSub is the central coordination mechanism. At least 10 distinct topics: terminal_presence, terminal_chat, blog_presence, bluesky:skeet, jetstream:skeet, relay:skeet, poke_around:firehose:events, poke_around:links:extracted, reddit_links, hacker_news:stories, map_updates, live_draft:{slug}, smart_steps:{session_id}. Each feature subscribes to its relevant topics. This is how 30+ LiveViews stay decoupled yet coordinated.

These handle ephemeral state (game sessions, rate limits, real-time feed data) where Postgres would be overkill. The SkeetStore module also manages its own ETS tables for Bluesky data buffering.

This is the most memorable feature because it has real physical consequences — a guest book that produces paper.
